violations.text: Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (slicer) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Staff cleaned and sanitized slicer during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/31/2025

Cheese and jam were observed held at 43-45 degrees in the prep units, with ambient temperatures at 47-51 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC adjusted thermostats during inspection. PIC stated service call was made. Inspector observed ambient temperatures were lowering during inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 07/31/2025

Working containers of liquid ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. New Violation. Correct By: 08/01/2025

Utensils (beverage strainers) in the bar are displayed with the lip surface up. Utensils are to be stored so that only the handles are touched by employees and consumers if consumer self-service is provided. New Violation. Correct By: 07/31/2025

Reach-in freezer unit on the cook line was observed in poor repair, observed with tape holding the plastic plate. PIC stated that the tape was the repair performed by the manufacturer's technician. Equipment components such as doors, seals, hinges, fasteners, and kick plates shall be kept intact, tight, and adjusted in accordance with manufacturer's specifications and be smooth, durable, and easily cleanable. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/02/2025

The door to the outside, located in the back hall, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 06/18/2025

Reach-in freezer unit on the cook line was observed in poor repair, observed with tape holding the plastic plate. PIC stated that the tape was the repair performed by the manufacturer's technician. Equipment components such as doors, seals, hinges, fasteners, and kick plates shall be kept intact, tight, and adjusted in accordance with manufacturer's specifications and be smooth, durable, and easily cleanable. New Violation. Correct By: 07/02/2025
